ogTitle: Daceyville vs Dairy Arm: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Daceyville and Dairy Arm.
heading: Daceyville vs Dairy Arm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Daceyville, New South Wales and Dairy Arm, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dairy Arm has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Daceyville (51/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Daceyville or Dairy Arm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Daceyville has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Dairy Arm scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Daceyville scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Dairy Arm scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Daceyville or Dairy Arm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dairy Arm has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 3.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
